eight_is_even.solution:
Theorem ev_8 : ev 8.
Proof.
  apply ev_SS. apply ev_SS. apply ev_SS. apply ev_SS. apply ev_0.
Qed.

Definition ev_8' : ev 8 :=
  ev_SS 6 (ev_SS 4 (ev_SS 2 (ev_SS 0 ev_0))).
eight_is_even.explanation: ev 8 is proved by applying ev_SS four times and then ev_0. The proof object chains the constructor applications.

conj_fact.solution:
Definition conj_fact : forall P Q R, P /\ Q -> Q /\ R -> P /\ R :=
  fun P Q R HPQ HQR =>
    match HPQ with
    | conj HP _ =>
      match HQR with
      | conj _ HR => conj HP HR
      end
    end.
conj_fact.explanation: Extract P from the first conjunction and R from the second, then combine them.

or_commut'.solution:
Definition or_commut' : forall P Q, P \/ Q -> Q \/ P :=
  fun P Q HPQ =>
    match HPQ with
    | or_introl HP => or_intror HP
    | or_intror HQ => or_introl HQ
    end.
or_commut'.explanation: Pattern match on the disjunction and swap the sides using or_introl and or_intror.

ex_ev_Sn.solution:
Definition ex_ev_Sn : ex (fun n => ev (S n)) :=
  ex_intro (fun n => ev (S n)) 1 (ev_SS 0 ev_0).
ex_ev_Sn.explanation: Witness n=1, then ev (S 1) = ev 2 = ev_SS 0 ev_0.

ex_match.solution:
Definition ex_match : forall (A : Type) (P Q : A -> Prop),
  (forall x, P x -> Q x) ->
  (exists x, P x) -> (exists x, Q x) :=
  fun A P Q HPQ Hex =>
    match Hex with
    | ex_intro _ x HPx => ex_intro Q x (HPQ x HPx)
    end.
ex_match.explanation: Destructure the existential to get witness x and proof of P x, then apply the implication to get Q x.

p_implies_true.solution:
Definition p_implies_true : forall P, P -> True :=
  fun P _ => I.
p_implies_true.explanation: Ignore the proof of P and return I, the constructor of True.

ex_falso_quodlibet'.solution:
Definition ex_falso_quodlibet' : forall P, False -> P :=
  fun P contra => match contra with end.
ex_falso_quodlibet'.explanation: Pattern match on False, which has no constructors, so the match is vacuously valid for any return type.

eq_cons.solution:
Definition eq_cons : forall (X : Type) (h1 h2 : X) (t1 t2 : list X),
    h1 == h2 -> t1 == t2 -> h1 :: t1 == h2 :: t2 :=
  fun X h1 h2 t1 t2 Hh Ht =>
    match Hh with
    | eq_refl h =>
      match Ht with
      | eq_refl t => eq_refl (h :: t)
      end
    end.
eq_cons.explanation: Pattern match on both equality proofs to unify h1=h2 and t1=t2, then use eq_refl on the cons.

equality__leibniz_equality.solution:
Lemma equality__leibniz_equality : forall (X : Type) (x y: X),
  x == y -> forall (P : X -> Prop), P x -> P y.
Proof.
  intros X x y Heq P HPx.
  destruct Heq as [a]. apply HPx.
Qed.
equality__leibniz_equality.explanation: Destruct the equality proof to unify x and y, then the goal P y becomes P x which is exactly our hypothesis.

equality__leibniz_equality_term.solution:
Definition equality__leibniz_equality_term : forall (X : Type) (x y: X),
    x == y -> forall P : (X -> Prop), P x -> P y :=
  fun X x y Heq =>
    match Heq with
    | eq_refl a => fun P HPa => HPa
    end.
equality__leibniz_equality_term.explanation: Pattern match on the equality proof immediately, unifying x and y to a, then the identity function suffices.

leibniz_equality__equality.solution:
Lemma leibniz_equality__equality : forall (X : Type) (x y: X),
  (forall P:X->Prop, P x -> P y) -> x == y.
Proof.
  intros X x y H.
  apply H. apply eq_refl.
Qed.
leibniz_equality__equality.explanation: Instantiate P with (fun z => x == z). Then P x is x == x which is eq_refl, and P y is x == y which is our goal.

and_assoc.solution:
Definition and_assoc : forall P Q R : Prop,
    P /\ (Q /\ R) -> (P /\ Q) /\ R :=
  fun P Q R H =>
    match H with
    | conj HP HQR =>
      match HQR with
      | conj HQ HR => conj (conj HP HQ) HR
      end
    end.
and_assoc.explanation: Destructure the nested conjunction, then reassociate the components.

or_distributes_over_and.solution:
Definition or_distributes_over_and : forall P Q R : Prop,
    P \/ (Q /\ R) <-> (P \/ Q) /\ (P \/ R) :=
  fun P Q R => conj
    (fun H => match H with
              | or_introl HP => conj (or_introl HP) (or_introl HP)
              | or_intror HQR =>
                match HQR with
                | conj HQ HR => conj (or_intror HQ) (or_intror HR)
                end
              end)
    (fun H => match H with
              | conj HPQ HPR =>
                match HPQ with
                | or_introl HP => or_introl HP
                | or_intror HQ =>
                  match HPR with
                  | or_introl HP => or_introl HP
                  | or_intror HR => or_intror (conj HQ HR)
                  end
                end
              end).
or_distributes_over_and.explanation: Both directions are proved by pattern matching on disjunctions and conjunctions, distributing or collecting as needed.

negations.solution:
Definition double_neg : forall P : Prop,
    P -> ~~P :=
  fun P HP HNP => HNP HP.

Definition contradiction_implies_anything : forall P Q : Prop,
    (P /\ ~P) -> Q :=
  fun P Q H =>
    match H with
    | conj HP HNP => match HNP HP with end
    end.

Definition de_morgan_not_or : forall P Q : Prop,
    ~ (P \/ Q) -> ~P /\ ~Q :=
  fun P Q H => conj
    (fun HP => H (or_introl HP))
    (fun HQ => H (or_intror HQ)).
negations.explanation: double_neg: apply the negation to the proof. contradiction: extract False from P and ~P. de_morgan: construct each negation by showing P or Q leads to a contradiction.

currying.solution:
Definition curry : forall P Q R : Prop,
    ((P /\ Q) -> R) -> (P -> (Q -> R)) :=
  fun P Q R H HP HQ => H (conj HP HQ).

Definition uncurry : forall P Q R : Prop,
    (P -> (Q -> R)) -> ((P /\ Q) -> R) :=
  fun P Q R H HPQ =>
    match HPQ with
    | conj HP HQ => H HP HQ
    end.
currying.explanation: curry: given P and Q separately, construct the pair and apply H. uncurry: destructure the pair and apply H to each component.

pe_implies_or_eq.solution:
Theorem pe_implies_or_eq :
  propositional_extensionality ->
  forall (P Q : Prop), (P \/ Q) = (Q \/ P).
Proof.
  intros PE P Q. apply PE.
  split.
  - intros [HP | HQ]. right. apply HP. left. apply HQ.
  - intros [HQ | HP]. right. apply HQ. left. apply HP.
Qed.
pe_implies_or_eq.explanation: Apply propositional extensionality, then prove both directions of the biconditional by swapping the disjunction.

pe_implies_true_eq.solution:
Lemma pe_implies_true_eq :
  propositional_extensionality ->
  forall (P : Prop), P -> True = P.
Proof.
  intros PE P HP. apply PE.
  split.
  - intros _. apply HP.
  - intros _. apply I.
Qed.
pe_implies_true_eq.explanation: Apply propositional extensionality. True -> P uses the given proof HP. P -> True is trivial.

pe_implies_pi.solution:
Theorem pe_implies_pi :
  propositional_extensionality -> proof_irrelevance.
Proof.
  unfold proof_irrelevance.
  intros PE P pf1 pf2.
  assert (H : True = P). { apply pe_implies_true_eq. apply PE. apply pf1. }
  subst P.
  destruct pf1. destruct pf2. reflexivity.
Qed.
pe_implies_pi.explanation: Use pe_implies_true_eq to show True = P, then substitute to make pf1, pf2 : True, and both must be I.
